summary: Clean, well-aligned DevOps resume that correctly honors the master-brief honesty rules (no fabricated 82% release or 80% provisioning claims) and surfaces the right AWS/K8s/CI-CD keywords for the Lucidity role.

issues.issue: The Healthcare project bullet uses 'zero-downtime redeployment' phrasing for what the master brief flags as a prototype/PoC handed off after client review. The brief warns against attaching production-scale outcomes to this project. While 'demonstrating zero-downtime redeployment for client review' matches the approved defensible bullet, 'zero-downtime' is a production reliability claim that is softer here but still borders on overstatement for a PoC. It does match the sanctioned bullet text, so this is low-risk.
issues.where: Healthcare project: "demonstrating zero-downtime redeployment for client review"
issues.suggestion: Acceptable as-is since it matches the approved brief bullet and is qualified with 'for client review'; optionally soften to 'demonstrating a zero-downtime redeployment approach' to further signal prototype scope.

issues.issue: Lucidity is a cloud cost-optimization / storage orchestration company (AutoScaler for block storage). The resume leads well with the 30% cost reduction, but does not surface storage-cost or block-storage/EBS optimization keywords that would resonate strongly with this specific employer's product domain. The Velero/EBS snapshot content exists but cost-of-storage framing is absent.
issues.where: Summary and experience bullets emphasize compute cost only: "cutting compute cost 30% ($500+/month)"
issues.suggestion: If defensible, add a phrase around storage/EBS cost or utilization awareness (e.g., resource utilization, EBS/volume management) to align with Lucidity's storage-cost-optimization product without fabricating new metrics.

issues.issue: colorScheme is set to 'teal' (startup/SaaS scheme). Master brief format rules mark Navy+gold enterprise/Cloud/SRE as DEFAULT, but teal is explicitly the startup/SaaS choice — Lucidity is a product startup, so teal is defensible. Noted only for awareness.
issues.suggestion: Teal is appropriate for a SaaS startup like Lucidity; no change required.

issues.issue: The last experience bullet is long and packs three distinct themes (RCA, observability, and security hardening) into one bullet, slightly breaking the parallel single-theme structure of the other bullets.
issues.where: "Drove root-cause analysis ... built observability ... hardened security with least-privilege IAM, Vault/Secrets Manager rotation, Sealed Secrets, and Trivy image scanning for audit-ready access controls."
issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into two bullets (RCA+observability, then security) if space allows, or trim the security clause for tighter one-to-two-line length.

issues.issue: Contact details (phone, email, LinkedIn, GitHub, location) are present in the source cv.md but not in the generated JSON payload — assuming the renderer injects the header from profile.yml, this is fine; flagging only to confirm the header is not dropped.
issues.where: Generated JSON has no header/contact block
issues.suggestion: Verify the rendered HTML includes name + phone + email + LinkedIn + GitHub + location from profile.yml.
